description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a steeply pitched, terracotta-tiled gable roof and a prominent stone chimney. The facade features a symmetrical arrangement of large rectangular windows, with two circular attic windows set into the roof gables. The exterior walls are finished in a weathered, light-colored stucco with visible stone trim and base, giving the house a rustic, aged appearance. A small balcony with a wooden railing is positioned above the main arched entry door, adding architectural interest to the front. The sides of the house are simpler, each with a single round window and minimal ornamentation. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional European and cottage influences, suitable for a suburban or village setting.
description: A two-story house with a terracotta-tiled gable roof, symmetrical windows, and a rustic stucco and stone exterior. Features include a balcony above the main door and circular attic windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched entry door, balcony with wooden railing, and symmetrical window arrangement with two circular attic windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep gable roof, stone chimney, and a single round window on each floor with minimal decoration.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with a central balcony and large windows, but lacking a visible entry door.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the gable roof, stone chimney, and round windows.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with circular attic windows
attribute_entryway_design: Arched wooden door with a small balcony above, no porch or steps, accented by stone trim
